## Architecture Reference

```
apps/web/src/
├── app/
│   ├── api/
│   │   ├── trpc/[trpc]/route.ts          ← tRPC handler (exists)
│   │   ├── ai/chat/route.ts              ← AI chat SSE (exists)
│   │   ├── webhooks/stripe/route.ts      ← Sprint 1 (CREATE)
│   │   ├── webhooks/resend/route.ts      ← Sprint 4 (CREATE)
│   │   ├── webhooks/whatsapp/route.ts    ← Sprint 5 (CREATE)
│   │   └── integrations/
│   │       ├── meta/callback/route.ts    ← Sprint 2 (CREATE)
│   │       ├── google/callback/route.ts  ← Sprint 3 (CREATE)
│   │       ├── tiktok/callback/route.ts  ← Sprint 5 (CREATE)
│   │       └── shopify/callback/route.ts ← Sprint 5 (CREATE)
│   └── dashboard/...                      ← Frontend (exists)
├── server/
│   ├── trpc/
│   │   ├── init.ts                        ← Context + middleware (exists)
│   │   └── routers/
│   │       ├── auth.ts                    ← EXISTS
│   │       ├── conversation.ts            ← EXISTS
│   │       ├── workflow.ts                ← EXISTS
│   │       ├── superadmin.ts              ← EXISTS
│   │       ├── contacts.ts               ← Sprint 1 (CREATE)
│   │       ├── deals.ts                   ← Sprint 1 (CREATE)
│   │       ├── pipeline.ts               ← Sprint 1 (CREATE)
│   │       ├── activities.ts             ← Sprint 1 (CREATE)
│   │       ├── billing.ts               ← Sprint 1 (CREATE)
│   │       ├── integrations.ts           ← Sprint 2 (CREATE)
│   │       ├── campaigns.ts              ← Sprint 2 (CREATE)
│   │       ├── health-score.ts           ← Sprint 3 (CREATE)
│   │       ├── email-campaigns.ts        ← Sprint 4 (CREATE)
│   │       ├── email-templates.ts        ← Sprint 4 (CREATE)
│   │       ├── sequences.ts             ← Sprint 4 (CREATE)
│   │       ├── whatsapp.ts              ← Sprint 5 (CREATE)
│   │       └── notifications.ts         ← Sprint 6 (CREATE)
│   ├── integrations/
│   │   ├── oauth.ts                      ← Sprint 2 (CREATE) — generic OAuth handler

│   │   │   ├── sync.ts                   ← Sprint 2 (CREATE)
│   │   │   └── capi.ts                   ← Sprint 2 (CREATE)
│   │   ├── google/
│   │   │   ├── auth.ts                   ← Sprint 3 (CREATE)
│   │   │   ├── ads-sync.ts              ← Sprint 3 (CREATE)
│   │   │   └── ga4-sync.ts              ← Sprint 3 (CREATE)
│   │   ├── whatsapp/
│   │   │   ├── client.ts                ← Sprint 5 (CREATE)
│   │   │   └── auto-reply.ts            ← Sprint 5 (CREATE)
│   │   ├── tiktok/
│   │   │   └── sync.ts                  ← Sprint 5 (CREATE)
│   │   └── shopify/
│   │       └── sync.ts                  ← Sprint 5 (CREATE)
│   ├── email/
│   │   └── resend.ts                    ← Sprint 4 (CREATE)
│   ├── stripe/

│   │   └── plans.ts                     ← Sprint 1 (CREATE)
│   └── ai/
│       ├── router.ts                    ← Sprint 6 (CREATE) — Bedrock model router
│       ├── cache.ts                     ← Sprint 6 (CREATE) — semantic cache
│       └── insights.ts                  ← Sprint 6 (CREATE) — pre-computed insights
│
apps/workers/src/triggers/
├── generate-health-score.ts             ← Sprint 3 (IMPLEMENT — currently skeleton)
├── sync-integration.ts                  ← Sprint 2 (IMPLEMENT — currently skeleton)
├── send-email.ts                        ← Sprint 4 (IMPLEMENT — currently skeleton)
```

## Existing Prisma Models (Ready to Use)

All models exist with tenant isolation (`tenantId` on every table). No schema migrations needed for Phase 1.

| Model | Key Fields | Used By Sprint |
|-------|-----------|---------------|
| Tenant | name, slug, plan, stripeCustomerId, trialEndsAt | All |
| User | email, name, roleId, isSuperAdmin, activeTenantId | All |
| Role | name, permissions (JSON), isSystem | Sprint 1 |
| Contact | firstName, lastName, email, phone, score, scoreLabel, tags, customData | Sprint 1 |
| Deal | contactId, pipelineId, title, value, stageId, probability, closedAt | Sprint 1 |
| Pipeline | name, stages (JSON: id, name, order, color), isDefault | Sprint 1 |
| Activity | contactId, type, subject, body, metadata | Sprint 1 |
| Integration | platform, accessToken, refreshToken, expiresAt, accountId, status, lastSyncAt | Sprint 2 |
| CampaignMetric | integrationId, platform, campaignId, campaignName, date, impressions, clicks, spend, conversions, revenue, ctr, cpc, roas | Sprint 2 |
| HealthScore | overallScore, adPerformance, contentEngagement, emailEffectiveness, leadConversion, revenueAttribution, insights, recommendations | Sprint 3 |
| Workflow | name, trigger, nodes, edges, isActive | Exists |
| EmailTemplate | name, subject, content (JSON), html | Sprint 4 |
| EmailCampaign | templateId, name, segmentQuery, scheduledAt, sentAt, status, stats | Sprint 4 |
| Message | contactId, channel (EMAIL/WHATSAPP/SMS), direction, content, metadata, status | Sprint 5 |
| Conversation | userId, title | Exists |
| ConversationMessage | conversationId, role, content, metadata | Exists |
| AiMemory | category, key, value, source | Sprint 6 |
| FieldDefinition | name, key, type, options, required, entity | Sprint 1 |
| PlatformAuditLog | userId, action, targetType, targetId, metadata, ip | Exists |

---

## Existing tRPC Middleware (Use These)

```typescript
// Public — no auth
publicProcedure

// Requires Supabase user
protectedProcedure → ctx: { user, supabase }

// Requires provisioned user + resolves tenant
tenantProcedure → ctx: { user, supabase, dbUser, effectiveTenantId }

// Requires isSuperAdmin
superAdminProcedure → ctx: { user, supabase, dbUser, effectiveTenantId }
```

**ALL new routers for tenant-scoped data MUST use `tenantProcedure` and filter by `ctx.effectiveTenantId`.**

---

## Sprint 1 — CRM Foundation + Billing (Weeks 1-2)

### 1.1 Contacts Router

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/contacts.ts`
**Middleware:** `tenantProcedure`

```
contacts.list
  Input: { page: number, limit: number, search?: string, scoreLabel?: ScoreLabel, tags?: string[] }
  Query: prisma.contact.findMany({ where: { tenantId: ctx.effectiveTenantId, ...filters }, orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' }, skip, take })
  Output: { contacts: Contact[], total: number, page: number, totalPages: number }

contacts.get
  Input: { contactId: string }
  Query: prisma.contact.findFirst({ where: { id: contactId, tenantId }, include: { deals: true, activities: { orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' }, take: 20 }, messages: { orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' }, take: 20 } } })
  Output: Contact with relations

contacts.create
  Input: { firstName: string, lastName?: string, email?: string, phone?: string, company?: string, source?: string, tags?: string[], customData?: Record<string, unknown> }
  Mutation: prisma.contact.create({ data: { tenantId: ctx.effectiveTenantId, ...input } })
  Side effect: Create Activity { type: 'contact_created', contactId }

contacts.update
  Input: { contactId: string, ...partial fields }
  Mutation: prisma.contact.update({ where: { id: contactId }, data: { ...input } })
  Guard: verify tenantId matches

contacts.delete
  Input: { contactId: string }
  Mutation: prisma.contact.delete (verify tenantId)

contacts.bulkImport
  Input: { contacts: Array<{ firstName, lastName?, email?, phone?, company?, source?, tags? }> }
  Mutation: prisma.contact.createMany({ data: contacts.map(c => ({ tenantId: ctx.effectiveTenantId, ...c })) })
  Output: { imported: number, skipped: number, errors: string[] }
  Logic: Skip duplicates (match by email within tenant), validate required fields

contacts.updateScore
  Input: { contactId: string, score: number, scoreLabel: ScoreLabel }
  Mutation: Update contact score + label
```

### 1.2 Deals Router

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/deals.ts`
**Middleware:** `tenantProcedure`

```
deals.list
  Input: { pipelineId?: string, stageId?: string, contactId?: string }
  Query: prisma.deal.findMany({ where: { tenantId, ...filters }, include: { contact: true, pipeline: true }, orderBy: { updatedAt: 'desc' } })
  Output: Deal[] with contact + pipeline

deals.get
  Input: { dealId: string }
  Output: Deal with contact + pipeline + activities

deals.create
  Input: { contactId: string, pipelineId: string, title: string, value?: number, stageId: string, probability?: number }
  Mutation: Create deal + Activity { type: 'deal_created' }

deals.update
  Input: { dealId: string, ...partial fields }
  Mutation: Update deal
  Side effect: If stageId changed → Activity { type: 'deal_stage_changed', metadata: { from, to } }

deals.close
  Input: { dealId: string, won: boolean }
  Mutation: Set closedAt + move to "Ganado" or "Perdido" stage
  Side effect: Activity { type: won ? 'deal_won' : 'deal_lost' }

deals.delete
  Input: { dealId: string }
```

### 1.3 Pipeline Router

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/pipeline.ts`
**Middleware:** `tenantProcedure`

```
pipeline.list
  Output: Pipeline[] (ordered by isDefault desc, name asc)

pipeline.get
  Input: { pipelineId: string }
  Output: Pipeline with deal counts per stage

pipeline.create
  Input: { name: string, stages: Array<{ name: string, color: string }> }
  Mutation: Create pipeline with auto-generated stage IDs and order

pipeline.update
  Input: { pipelineId: string, name?: string, stages?: Array<{ id: string, name: string, order: number, color: string }> }
  Mutation: Update pipeline

pipeline.delete
  Input: { pipelineId: string }
  Guard: Cannot delete default pipeline
```

### 1.4 Activities Router

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/activities.ts`
**Middleware:** `tenantProcedure`

```
activities.list
  Input: { contactId?: string, type?: string, page: number, limit: number }
  Output: { activities: Activity[], total, page, totalPages }

activities.create
  Input: { contactId: string, type: string, subject?: string, body?: string, metadata?: any }
  Mutation: Create activity with createdBy = ctx.dbUser.id

activities.delete
  Input: { activityId: string }
```

### 1.5 Stripe Billing

**Install:** `pnpm --filter web add stripe @stripe/stripe-js`

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/stripe/client.ts`
```typescript
import Stripe from 'stripe';
export const stripe = new Stripe(process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY!);
```

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/stripe/plans.ts`
```typescript
export const PLAN_PRICES: Record<Plan, string> = {
  INICIO: 'price_xxx_inicio',        // $39/mo
  CRECIMIENTO: 'price_xxx_crec',     // $99/mo
  PROFESIONAL: 'price_xxx_pro',      // $199/mo
  AGENCIA: 'price_xxx_agencia',      // $299/mo
};

export const PLAN_FEATURES: Record<Plan, { maxContacts: number, maxEmails: number, aiTier: string, mediaGeneration: boolean }> = {
  INICIO:       { maxContacts: 500,   maxEmails: 5000,   aiTier: 'haiku',  mediaGeneration: false },
  CRECIMIENTO:  { maxContacts: 5000,  maxEmails: 25000,  aiTier: 'sonnet', mediaGeneration: false },
  PROFESIONAL:  { maxContacts: 25000, maxEmails: 100000, aiTier: 'opus',   mediaGeneration: true },
  AGENCIA:      { maxContacts: -1,    maxEmails: -1,     aiTier: 'opus',   mediaGeneration: true },
};
```

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/billing.ts`
**Middleware:** `tenantProcedure`

```
billing.getSubscription
  Query: Fetch Stripe subscription via tenant.stripeCustomerId
  Output: { plan, status, currentPeriodEnd, cancelAtPeriodEnd, paymentMethod }

billing.createCheckout
  Input: { plan: Plan }
  Mutation: stripe.checkout.sessions.create({ customer, price, success_url, cancel_url })
  Output: { url: string } — redirect to Stripe checkout

billing.createPortalSession
  Mutation: stripe.billingPortal.sessions.create({ customer, return_url })
  Output: { url: string } — redirect to Stripe portal (manage subscription)

billing.getUsage
  Query: Count contacts, emails sent, AI requests for current tenant
  Output: { contacts: { used, limit }, emails: { used, limit }, aiRequests: { used, limit } }
```

**File:** `apps/web/src/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts`
```
POST handler:
  - Verify Stripe signature (stripe.webhooks.constructEvent)
  - Handle events:
    checkout.session.completed → Set tenant plan + stripeCustomerId
    customer.subscription.updated → Update tenant plan if changed
    customer.subscription.deleted → Downgrade to INICIO
    invoice.payment_failed → Mark subscription as past_due, send notification
```

### 1.6 Plan Enforcement Middleware

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/trpc/middleware/plan-gate.ts`

```typescript
export function requirePlan(minimumPlan: Plan) {
  return tenantProcedure.use(async ({ ctx, next }) => {
    const tenant = await prisma.tenant.findUnique({ where: { id: ctx.effectiveTenantId } });
    const planOrder = { INICIO: 0, CRECIMIENTO: 1, PROFESIONAL: 2, AGENCIA: 3 };
    if (planOrder[tenant.plan] < planOrder[minimumPlan]) {
      throw new TRPCError({ code: 'FORBIDDEN', message: `Requiere plan ${minimumPlan} o superior` });
    }
    return next({ ctx });
  });
}
```

Usage: `workflow.create` → `requirePlan('CRECIMIENTO')` (INICIO can't save workflows)

### 1.7 Wire CRM to Frontend

Replace mock data in these sidebar pages with real tRPC queries:
- `components/pages/contacts-pages.tsx` → `TodosContactosPage` uses `trpc.contacts.list`
- `components/pages/contacts-pages.tsx` → `PipelinePage` uses `trpc.deals.list` grouped by stage
- `components/pages/contacts-pages.tsx` → `DealsPage` uses `trpc.deals.list`
- `components/pages/contacts-pages.tsx` → `ActividadesPage` uses `trpc.activities.list`

### 1.8 Register New Routers

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/_app.ts` (or wherever the app router is)
Add: contacts, deals, pipeline, activities, billing

## Sprint 2 — Meta Ads Integration + Data Sync (Weeks 3-4)

### 2.1 Generic OAuth Framework

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/integrations/oauth.ts`

```typescript
interface OAuthConfig {
  provider: string;
  authUrl: string;
  tokenUrl: string;
  clientId: string;
  clientSecret: string;
  scopes: string[];
  redirectUri: string;
}

export function getAuthorizationUrl(config: OAuthConfig, state: string): string
export async function exchangeCodeForTokens(config: OAuthConfig, code: string): Promise<{ accessToken, refreshToken?, expiresAt? }>
export async function refreshAccessToken(config: OAuthConfig, refreshToken: string): Promise<{ accessToken, expiresAt }>
```

**State parameter:** Encrypt `{ tenantId, userId, provider }` as JWT → pass as `state` in OAuth URL → verify on callback.

### 2.2 Meta Ads OAuth

**Install:** `pnpm --filter web add facebook-nodejs-business-sdk`

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/integrations/meta/auth.ts`

```
Config:
  authUrl: https://www.facebook.com/v21.0/dialog/oauth
  tokenUrl: https://graph.facebook.com/v21.0/oauth/access_token
  scopes: ads_read, ads_management, pages_read_engagement, pages_manage_posts
  clientId: process.env.META_APP_ID
  clientSecret: process.env.META_APP_SECRET

Flow:
  1. User clicks "Conectar Meta Ads" in integrations page
  2. Call trpc.integrations.startOAuth({ provider: 'meta' })
  3. Backend generates auth URL with state JWT → returns URL
  4. Frontend redirects to Meta
  5. User authorizes → Meta redirects to /api/integrations/meta/callback?code=xxx&state=xxx
  6. Callback handler:
     a. Verify state JWT → extract tenantId, userId
     b. Exchange code for short-lived token
     c. Exchange for long-lived token (60 days)
     d. Get ad accounts via /me/adaccounts
     e. Store in Integration model: { tenantId, platform: 'meta_ads', accessToken, accountId: adAccountId, expiresAt }
     f. Create Activity log
     g. Trigger initial sync
     h. Redirect to /dashboard (integrations page)
```

**File:** `apps/web/src/app/api/integrations/meta/callback/route.ts`
- GET handler implementing the callback flow above

### 2.3 Meta Ads Sync Worker

**File:** `apps/workers/src/triggers/sync-meta.ts`

```
Task: sync-meta
Payload: { tenantId: string, integrationId: string }

Steps:
  1. Fetch Integration record → get accessToken, accountId
  2. Check token expiry → refresh if needed → update Integration
  3. Call Meta Marketing API:
     GET /{adAccountId}/campaigns?fields=id,name,status,objective
     GET /{adAccountId}/insights?fields=impressions,clicks,spend,actions,action_values
       &time_range={'since': 30 days ago, 'until': today}
       &time_increment=1  (daily breakdown)
       &level=campaign
  4. For each campaign × date:
     Upsert CampaignMetric: {
       tenantId, integrationId, platform: 'meta_ads',
       campaignId, campaignName, date,
       impressions, clicks, spend,
       conversions: (actions where type='offsite_conversion.fb_pixel_lead'),
       revenue: (action_values where type='offsite_conversion.fb_pixel_purchase'),
       ctr: clicks/impressions, cpc: spend/clicks, roas: revenue/spend
     }
  5. Update Integration.lastSyncAt
  6. Log success/failure

Schedule: Run every 6 hours via Trigger.dev cron
```

### 2.4 Meta CAPI

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/integrations/meta/capi.ts`

```typescript
export async function sendConversionEvent(params: {
  tenantId: string;
  event: 'Lead' | 'Purchase' | 'CompleteRegistration' | 'AddToCart';
  userData: { email?: string; phone?: string; firstName?: string; lastName?: string };
  customData?: { value?: number; currency?: string; content_ids?: string[] };
}) {
  // Hash user data (SHA256) per Meta requirements
  // POST to /{pixelId}/events with server event
}
```

Triggered from: deal stage changes (Lead → Purchase), form submissions (Lead), email clicks (ViewContent)

### 2.5 Integrations tRPC Router

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/integrations.ts`
**Middleware:** `tenantProcedure`

```
integrations.list
  Output: Integration[] for tenant (hide accessToken/refreshToken in response)

integrations.startOAuth
  Input: { provider: 'meta_ads' | 'google_ads' | 'ga4' | 'tiktok' | 'shopify' }
  Output: { authUrl: string }
  Logic: Generate OAuth URL with state JWT

integrations.disconnect
  Input: { integrationId: string }
  Mutation: Delete Integration record

integrations.syncNow
  Input: { integrationId: string }
  Mutation: Trigger sync worker immediately
  Output: { triggered: true }

integrations.getSyncStatus
  Input: { integrationId: string }
  Output: { status, lastSyncAt, error? }
```

### 2.6 Campaigns tRPC Router

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/campaigns.ts`
**Middleware:** `tenantProcedure`

```
campaigns.listMetrics
  Input: { platform?: string, dateRange: { from: Date, to: Date }, groupBy?: 'day' | 'week' | 'month' }
  Query: Aggregate CampaignMetric records
  Output: { metrics: Array<{ date, impressions, clicks, spend, conversions, revenue, ctr, cpc, roas }>, totals: { ... } }

campaigns.listCampaigns
  Input: { platform?: string }
  Query: Distinct campaigns from CampaignMetric with aggregated stats
  Output: Array<{ campaignId, campaignName, platform, totalSpend, totalConversions, roas, lastDate }>

campaigns.getChannelComparison
  Output: Array<{ platform, spend, revenue, leads, roas }> — one row per connected platform
```

### 2.7 Wire Frontend

- Integrations page: Show real `trpc.integrations.list`, "Conectar" triggers `startOAuth`, show sync status
- Campaigns pages: Real data from `trpc.campaigns.listMetrics` and `listCampaigns`
- Dashboard Home stats: Pull from campaign metrics aggregation

## Sprint 3 — Google Ads + GA4 + Health Score (Weeks 5-6)

### 3.1 Google Ads OAuth

**Install:** `pnpm --filter web add google-ads-api googleapis`

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/integrations/google/auth.ts`

```
Config:
  authUrl: https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth
  tokenUrl: https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token
  scopes: https://www.googleapis.com/auth/adwords (Google Ads)
          https://www.googleapis.com/auth/analytics.readonly (GA4)
  clientId: process.env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID
  clientSecret: process.env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET

Flow: Same as Meta but:
  - Access type: offline (for refresh token)
  - Prompt: consent (force re-consent for refresh token)
  - After token exchange: call /v2/userinfo to get Google user info
  - For Ads: get accessible customer IDs via CustomerService
  - For GA4: get accessible properties via AdminService
  - Store separate Integration records for google_ads and ga4
```

### 3.2 Google Ads Sync Worker

**File:** `apps/workers/src/triggers/sync-google-ads.ts`

```
Task: sync-google-ads
Payload: { tenantId, integrationId }

Steps:
  1. Fetch Integration → get refreshToken, accountId (customer ID)
  2. Initialize GoogleAdsApi client with developer token + refresh token
  3. Query campaigns:
     SELECT campaign.id, campaign.name, campaign.status,
            metrics.impressions, metrics.clicks, metrics.cost_micros,
            metrics.conversions, metrics.conversions_value,
            segments.date
     FROM campaign
     WHERE segments.date DURING LAST_30_DAYS
  4. For each row: upsert CampaignMetric (platform: 'google_ads')
     Note: cost_micros → divide by 1,000,000 for dollars
  5. Update Integration.lastSyncAt
```

### 3.3 GA4 Sync Worker

**File:** `apps/workers/src/triggers/sync-ga4.ts`

```
Task: sync-ga4
Payload: { tenantId, integrationId }

Steps:
  1. Fetch Integration → get refreshToken, accountId (property ID)
  2. Initialize BetaAnalyticsDataClient with credentials
  3. Run reports:
     Metrics: sessions, totalUsers, newUsers, screenPageViews, conversions, engagementRate, bounceRate
     Dimensions: date, sessionSource, sessionMedium, sessionCampaignName
     Date range: last 30 days
  4. Store in CampaignMetric with platform: 'ga4'
  5. Update Integration.lastSyncAt
```

### 3.4 Health Score Algorithm

**File:** `apps/workers/src/triggers/generate-health-score.ts`

```
Task: generate-health-score
Payload: { tenantId }
Schedule: Every 6 hours

Algorithm — 5 pillars, each scored 0-100:

1. adPerformance (weight: 25%)
   - Has active ad integrations? (+20 if yes, 0 if no)
   - Average ROAS across campaigns (+0-40 based on ROAS: 0x=0, 2x=20, 4x=40)
   - Spend trend (increasing = +20, stable = +10, decreasing = 0)
   - Data freshness (last sync < 24h = +20, < 48h = +10, else 0)

2. contentEngagement (weight: 20%)
   - Email open rate (+0-30: 0%=0, 15%=15, 30%+=30)
   - Email click rate (+0-30: 0%=0, 2%=15, 5%+=30)
   - Social engagement if connected (+0-20)
   - WhatsApp response rate (+0-20)

3. emailEffectiveness (weight: 20%)
   - Has email templates? (+10)
   - Sent campaigns in last 30 days? (+20)
   - Active sequences? (+20)
   - Bounce rate < 2%? (+10)
   - Unsubscribe rate < 0.5%? (+10)
   - Deliverability rate > 95%? (+30)

4. leadConversion (weight: 20%)
   - Has contacts? (+10)
   - Pipeline conversion rate (+0-30: deals won / total deals)
   - Average deal cycle time (faster = higher)
   - Lead scoring active? (+10)
   - Contact completeness (% with email + phone) (+0-20)

5. revenueAttribution (weight: 15%)
   - Stripe connected? (+20)
   - Can attribute revenue to channels? (+30)
   - ROAS trackable? (+20)
   - Revenue trend positive? (+30)

Overall = weighted average of 5 pillars

Store:
  prisma.healthScore.create({
    data: {
      tenantId, overallScore, adPerformance, contentEngagement,
      emailEffectiveness, leadConversion, revenueAttribution,
      insights: AI_GENERATED (or computed summary),
      recommendations: [top 3 actionable items]
    }
  })
```

### 3.5 Health Score Router

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/health-score.ts`

```
healthScore.getCurrent
  Output: Latest HealthScore for tenant (or null if not calculated yet)

healthScore.getHistory
  Input: { days: number (default 30) }
  Output: HealthScore[] ordered by calculatedAt (for trend sparklines)

healthScore.recalculate
  Mutation: Trigger generate-health-score worker immediately
```

### 3.6 Wire Node Map to Real Data

Update `NodeMapPage` in `dashboard-pages.tsx`:
- Query `trpc.integrations.list` → create nodes for each connected integration
- Query `trpc.integrations.getSyncStatus` → color nodes by status (healthy/warning/error)
- Node positions can still be hardcoded by platform type (ads top, CRM center, etc.)
- AI banner pulls from latest `healthScore.insights`

---

## Sprint 4 — Email Engine + Campaign Management (Weeks 7-8)

### 4.1 Email Service

**Install:** `pnpm --filter web add resend react-email @react-email/components`

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/email/resend.ts`

```typescript
import { Resend } from 'resend';

const resend = new Resend(process.env.RESEND_API_KEY);

export async function sendTransactionalEmail(params: {
  to: string;
  subject: string;
  html: string;
  from?: string;
  replyTo?: string;
  tags?: { name: string; value: string }[];
}): Promise<{ id: string }>

export async function sendBulkEmails(params: {
  emails: Array<{ to: string; subject: string; html: string }>;
}): Promise<{ sent: number; failed: number }>
```

### 4.2 Send Email Worker

**File:** `apps/workers/src/triggers/send-email.ts`

```
Task: send-email
Payload: {
  tenantId: string;
  campaignId?: string;
  to: string | string[];   // single or batch
  subject: string;
  html: string;
  mergeFields?: Record<string, Record<string, string>>; // { "email@x.com": { nombre: "Juan" } }
}

Steps:
  1. If mergeFields: replace {{field}} placeholders in subject + html per recipient
  2. Call resend.sendTransactionalEmail or sendBulkEmails
  3. If campaignId: update EmailCampaign.stats (increment sent count)
  4. Create Message record per recipient (channel: EMAIL, direction: OUTBOUND)
  5. Return { sent, failed }
```

### 4.3 Email Templates Router

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/email-templates.ts`

```
emailTemplates.list
  Output: EmailTemplate[] for tenant

emailTemplates.get
  Input: { templateId }
  Output: EmailTemplate

emailTemplates.create
  Input: { name, subject, content (JSON from email builder blocks) }
  Mutation: Create template + render content JSON to HTML (store in html field)

emailTemplates.update
  Input: { templateId, name?, subject?, content? }
  Mutation: Update + re-render HTML

emailTemplates.delete
  Input: { templateId }

emailTemplates.preview
  Input: { templateId, mergeFields?: Record<string, string> }
  Output: { html: string } — rendered HTML with merge fields applied
```

### 4.4 Email Campaigns Router

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/email-campaigns.ts`

```
emailCampaigns.list
  Output: EmailCampaign[] with template info

emailCampaigns.create
  Input: { name, templateId, segmentQuery?: JSON (contact filters), scheduledAt?: Date }
  Mutation: Create campaign with status 'draft'

emailCampaigns.send
  Input: { campaignId }
  Mutation:
    1. Fetch contacts matching segmentQuery (or all contacts if no query)
    2. Render template with merge fields per contact
    3. Trigger send-email worker (batch)
    4. Update campaign status to 'sent', sentAt to now()
  Guard: requirePlan('CRECIMIENTO')

emailCampaigns.schedule
  Input: { campaignId, scheduledAt: Date }
  Mutation: Update status to 'scheduled'

emailCampaigns.getStats
  Input: { campaignId }
  Output: { sent, delivered, opened, clicked, bounced, complained }
```

### 4.5 Resend Webhook Handler

**File:** `apps/web/src/app/api/webhooks/resend/route.ts`

```
POST handler:
  - Verify Resend webhook signature
  - Handle events:
    email.delivered → update Message status + campaign stats
    email.opened → increment campaign opens + create Activity
    email.clicked → increment campaign clicks + create Activity + update contact score (+5)
    email.bounced → update Message status + flag contact
    email.complained → update Message status + unsubscribe contact
```

### 4.6 Drip Sequences

**New Prisma model needed (add to schema):**
```prisma
model Sequence {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  tenantId  String
  name      String
  steps     Json     // [{ order, templateId, delayHours, condition? }]
  isActive  Boolean  @default(false)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  tenant    Tenant   @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
  @@index([tenantId])
}

model SequenceEnrollment {
  id         String   @id @default(uuid())
  sequenceId String
  contactId  String
  currentStep Int     @default(0)
  status     String   @default("active") // active, completed, unsubscribed
  nextSendAt DateTime?
  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())
  @@index([sequenceId, status])
  @@index([nextSendAt])
}
```

**File:** `apps/workers/src/triggers/drip-sequence.ts`

```
Task: drip-sequence-processor
Schedule: Every 15 minutes

Steps:
  1. Query SequenceEnrollment where status='active' AND nextSendAt <= now()
  2. For each enrollment:
     a. Get sequence → get current step
     b. Render template for contact
     c. Trigger send-email worker
     d. Increment currentStep
     e. If more steps: set nextSendAt = now() + next step's delayHours
     f. If no more steps: set status = 'completed'
```

---

## Sprint 5 — WhatsApp + Contact Import + More Integrations (Weeks 9-10)

### 5.1 WhatsApp Cloud API

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/integrations/whatsapp/client.ts`

```typescript
const WHATSAPP_API = 'https://graph.facebook.com/v21.0';

export async function sendTextMessage(phoneNumberId: string, to: string, text: string, accessToken: string)
export async function sendTemplateMessage(phoneNumberId: string, to: string, templateName: string, params: string[], accessToken: string)
export async function markAsRead(phoneNumberId: string, messageId: string, accessToken: string)
```

**File:** `apps/web/src/app/api/webhooks/whatsapp/route.ts`

```
GET handler: Webhook verification (verify_token challenge)

POST handler:
  - Parse incoming message payload
  - Extract: from (phone), message text, message type, timestamp
  - Find contact by phone in tenant
  - Create Message record (channel: WHATSAPP, direction: INBOUND)
  - Check auto-reply rules → send response if match
  - Emit real-time notification (Supabase Realtime or polling)
```

### 5.2 WhatsApp Router

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/whatsapp.ts`

```
whatsapp.listConversations
  Query: Distinct contacts with WhatsApp messages, ordered by latest message

whatsapp.getMessages
  Input: { contactId }
  Output: Message[] where channel=WHATSAPP, ordered by createdAt

whatsapp.send
  Input: { contactId, message: string }
  Mutation: Send via WhatsApp Cloud API + create Message record

whatsapp.sendTemplate
  Input: { contactId, templateName, params }
  Mutation: Send template message

whatsapp.broadcast
  Input: { contactIds: string[], templateName, params }
  Mutation: Send to multiple contacts (throttled)
  Guard: requirePlan('PROFESIONAL')
```

### 5.3 Auto-Reply Rules

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/integrations/whatsapp/auto-reply.ts`

```typescript
interface AutoReplyRule {
  keywords: string[];       // trigger words
  response: string;         // response text
  businessHoursOnly: boolean;
  enabled: boolean;
}

// Store rules as JSON on Integration.metadata
// Check incoming messages against rules
// If match: send auto-reply + create Message record
```

### 5.4 Contact Import (CSV)

**File:** `apps/web/src/components/ui/contact-import-wizard.tsx`

```
Steps:
  1. Upload CSV file (client-side parsing with Papa Parse)
  2. Field mapping UI: map CSV columns to Contact fields (firstName, lastName, email, phone, etc.)
  3. Preview: show first 5 rows with mapped fields
  4. Deduplication check: match by email within tenant
  5. Import: call trpc.contacts.bulkImport
  6. Result: show imported count, skipped duplicates, errors

Install: pnpm --filter web add papaparse @types/papaparse
```

### 5.5 TikTok Ads Integration

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/integrations/tiktok/sync.ts`

Same pattern as Meta:
- OAuth via TikTok Marketing API
- Sync campaigns, ad groups, ads metrics
- Store in CampaignMetric with platform: 'tiktok'

### 5.6 Shopify Integration

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/integrations/shopify/sync.ts`

- OAuth via Shopify Admin API
- Pull: orders (last 30 days), products, customers
- Store orders as CampaignMetric with platform: 'shopify' (revenue attribution)
- Map Shopify customers to contacts (match by email)

---

## Sprint 6 — AI Intelligence + Reports + Launch Polish (Weeks 11-12)

### 6.1 Bedrock AI Router

**Install:** `pnpm --filter web add @aws-sdk/client-bedrock-runtime @aws-sdk/client-bedrock`

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/ai/router.ts`

```typescript
import { BedrockRuntimeClient, InvokeModelCommand } from '@aws-sdk/client-bedrock-runtime';

const bedrock = new BedrockRuntimeClient({ region: 'us-east-1' });

type Tier = 'haiku' | 'sonnet' | 'opus';

const MODEL_IDS: Record<Tier, string> = {
  haiku: 'anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0',
  sonnet: 'anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6-20250514-v1:0',
  opus: 'anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-20250514-v1:0',
};

export async function classifyIntent(message: string): Promise<Tier> {
  // Use Haiku to classify the complexity of the request
  // Simple lookup/format → haiku
  // Analysis/summary → sonnet
  // Strategy/generation/cross-domain → opus
}

export async function routeAndInvoke(params: {
  message: string;
  context: string;     // tenant data summary
  tier?: Tier;         // override tier
  stream?: boolean;
}): Promise<string | ReadableStream>
```

### 6.2 Semantic Cache

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/ai/cache.ts`

```typescript
import { BedrockRuntimeClient, InvokeModelCommand } from '@aws-sdk/client-bedrock-runtime';

// Use Titan Embeddings via Bedrock for embedding
// Store embeddings in pgvector column on AiMemory model

export async function embedQuery(query: string): Promise<number[]>
export async function findCachedResponse(tenantId: string, query: string, threshold: number = 0.92): Promise<string | null>
export async function cacheResponse(tenantId: string, query: string, response: string): Promise<void>
```

**Schema change needed:**
```prisma
// Add to AiMemory or create new model
model AiCache {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  tenantId  String
  query     String
  embedding Unsupported("vector(1024)") // pgvector
  response  String
  hits      Int      @default(0)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  @@index([tenantId])
}
```

Enable pgvector extension in Supabase.

### 6.3 Pre-computed Insights

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/ai/insights.ts`

```
Function: computeTenantInsights(tenantId)
Schedule: Every 6 hours (via Trigger.dev, after health score)

Computes and caches:
  - topChannel: best ROAS channel with metrics
  - worstChannel: worst ROAS channel
  - pipelineBottleneck: stage with lowest conversion rate
  - leadTrend: up/down/flat with percentage
  - revenueTrend: up/down/flat with percentage
  - anomalies: any metric that deviated >20% from 7-day average
  - recommendations: top 3 actionable items

Store in AiMemory { tenantId, category: 'insights', key: 'latest', value: JSON }

When user asks common questions, return pre-computed answer instead of calling LLM.
```

### 6.4 PDF Report Generation

**Install:** `pnpm --filter web add @react-pdf/renderer`

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/reports/pdf-generator.ts`

```typescript
import { renderToBuffer } from '@react-pdf/renderer';

export async function generateReport(params: {
  tenantId: string;
  tenantName: string;
  dateRange: { from: Date; to: Date };
  sections: ('summary' | 'channels' | 'funnel' | 'campaigns' | 'recommendations')[];
}): Promise<Buffer>

// React-PDF document with:
// - Cover page with tenant logo + date range
// - Executive summary (KPI cards)
// - Channel performance table + chart (rendered as SVG)
// - Funnel visualization
// - Campaign breakdown
// - AI recommendations
// - Footer: "Generado por NodeLabz"
```

### 6.5 Scheduled Reports Worker

**File:** `apps/workers/src/triggers/scheduled-report.ts`

```
Task: scheduled-report
Schedule: Daily at 7am, Weekly on Mondays, Monthly on 1st

Steps:
  1. Query tenants with scheduled reports configured
  2. For each: generate PDF via pdf-generator
  3. Send via email (Resend) to configured recipients
  4. Store report reference for download in dashboard
```

### 6.6 AI Daily Digest Worker

**File:** `apps/workers/src/triggers/ai-digest.ts`

```
Task: ai-digest
Schedule: Daily at 8am (per tenant timezone)

Steps:
  1. For each active tenant with CRECIMIENTO+ plan:
  2. Gather last 24h data: new leads, campaign metrics changes, deals moved, emails sent
  3. Call Bedrock Sonnet with data summary → generate digest
  4. Store as notification + optionally email
```

### 6.7 Notification System

**New Prisma model:**
```prisma
model Notification {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  tenantId  String
  userId    String?   // null = all users in tenant
  type      String    // 'ai_digest', 'integration_error', 'lead_alert', 'health_score_drop'
  title     String
  body      String
  metadata  Json?
  read      Boolean  @default(false)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  @@index([tenantId, userId, read])
}
```

**File:** `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/notifications.ts`

```
notifications.list
  Input: { unreadOnly?: boolean }
  Output: Notification[] (last 50)

notifications.markRead
  Input: { notificationId: string }

notifications.markAllRead
```

### 6.8 Onboarding Wizard

**File:** `apps/web/src/components/ui/onboarding-wizard.tsx`

```
Steps:
  1. "Bienvenido a NodeLabz" — explain the platform
  2. "Conecta tu primer canal" — show integration cards (Meta, Google, etc.)
  3. "Importa tus contactos" — CSV upload or skip
  4. "Configura tu pipeline" — customize default stages or keep defaults
  5. "Crea tu primera automatizacion" — simple workflow template
  6. "Listo!" — show health score + next steps

Track progress: Store wizard step in User.preferences JSON
Show wizard: If user.preferences.onboardingComplete !== true
```

### 6.9 Higgsfield Media Generation

**Install:** `pnpm --filter web add higgsfield`

**File:** `apps/workers/src/triggers/generate-media.ts`

```
Task: generate-media
Payload: { tenantId, type: 'image' | 'video', prompt: string, options?: { model?, style?, duration? } }

Steps:
  1. Check tenant plan (requirePlan PROFESIONAL for images, AGENCIA for video)
  2. Check tenant credit balance
  3. Call Higgsfield API via SDK
  4. Poll for completion
  5. Upload result to Cloudflare R2
  6. Return URL
  7. Deduct credits from tenant
```

### 6.10 Launch Polish

```
- Error boundaries on all pages
- Loading skeletons for every tRPC query (use Suspense or isLoading states)
- Empty states: "No tienes contactos aun" with CTA to import
- 404 page styled with dark theme
- Rate limiting on API routes (use Upstash Redis or in-memory)
- Input validation on all tRPC inputs (Zod schemas)
- XSS prevention: sanitize user HTML in email templates
- CORS configuration
- Environment variable validation on startup
- Sentry or similar error tracking
```

---

## New Schema Migrations Needed

Only 2 new models + 1 extension required beyond what exists:

```prisma
// 1. Drip sequences
model Sequence { ... }           // Sprint 4
model SequenceEnrollment { ... } // Sprint 4

// 2. Notifications
model Notification { ... }       // Sprint 6

// 3. AI Cache (pgvector)
model AiCache { ... }            // Sprint 6
// + enable pgvector extension in Supabase
```

---

## Package Installations Summary

| Sprint | Package | Purpose |
|--------|---------|---------|
| 1 | `stripe`, `@stripe/stripe-js` | Billing |
| 2 | `facebook-nodejs-business-sdk` | Meta Ads API |
| 3 | `google-ads-api`, `googleapis` | Google Ads + GA4 |
| 4 | `resend`, `react-email`, `@react-email/components` | Email sending |
| 5 | `papaparse`, `@types/papaparse` | CSV import |
| 6 | `@aws-sdk/client-bedrock-runtime` | AI model routing |
| 6 | `@react-pdf/renderer` | PDF reports |
| 6 | `higgsfield` | Image/video generation |

## Environment Variables Needed

```env
# Stripe (Sprint 1)
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_xxx
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_xxx
NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_xxx

# Meta (Sprint 2)
META_APP_ID=xxx
META_APP_SECRET=xxx
META_PIXEL_ID=xxx

# Google (Sprint 3)
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=xxx
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=xxx
GOOGLE_ADS_DEVELOPER_TOKEN=xxx

# Resend (Sprint 4)
RESEND_API_KEY=re_xxx
RESEND_WEBHOOK_SECRET=xxx
EMAIL_FROM=notifications@nodelabz.app

# WhatsApp (Sprint 5)
WHATSAPP_PHONE_NUMBER_ID=xxx
WHATSAPP_ACCESS_TOKEN=xxx
WHATSAPP_VERIFY_TOKEN=xxx

# TikTok (Sprint 5)
TIKTOK_APP_ID=xxx
TIKTOK_APP_SECRET=xxx

# Shopify (Sprint 5)
SHOPIFY_API_KEY=xxx
SHOPIFY_API_SECRET=xxx

# AWS Bedrock (Sprint 6)
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=xxx
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=xxx
AWS_REGION=us-east-1

# Higgsfield (Sprint 6)
HIGGSFIELD_KEY_ID=xxx
HIGGSFIELD_KEY_SECRET=xxx

# Existing
SUPABASE_URL=xxx
SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=xxx
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=xxx
AI_SERVICE_URL=http://localhost:8000
AI_SERVICE_KEY=xxx
TRIGGER_DEV_API_KEY=xxx
```

| New tRPC routers | 13 |

| New worker tasks | 8 |

*This document is the complete Phase 1 implementation guide for NodeLabz. Every file path, every function signature, every data flow is specified. A developer can pick up any sprint and start building without ambiguity. Locked March 16, 2026.*
